title: Compliance & Approved Language
modulePurpose:
Say It Right, Sell It Right, Protect Yourself

Section Purpose
This section teaches agents how to communicate clearly, honestly, and confidently about MightyWELL products.
Compliance is not about being careful.
Compliance is about being clear.
The most successful agents are not the ones who try to sound like insurance.
They are the ones who confidently explain what this is.
Agents should leave this section understanding:
we are not selling insurance
we never present the bundle as traditional insurance
we always explain that this is a healthcare bundle
we use the correct terminology
we are completely transparent with customers
honesty builds trust — and trust builds long-term business
The goal of this module is simple:
Say it right. Sell it right. Stay protected.

moduleSteps.title: Step 1 – Review Overview
moduleSteps.subtitle: Review Overview
moduleSteps.copy:
The Most Important Rule
Never tell a customer they are buying insurance.
Not accidentally.
 Not casually.
 Not "kind of."
Never.
MightyWELL is not an insurance company.
You are not selling insurance.
You are offering a healthcare bundle — an alternative to traditional insurance.
That distinction matters.
And the best way to handle it is not to hide it.
The best way is to be confident about it.

The Right Mindset for Salespeople
Some salespeople get nervous about this.
They think:
"If I tell them this isn't insurance, they won't buy."
That is not true.
The opposite is true.
Customers leave when they feel misled.
Customers stay when they understand exactly what they bought.
Transparency builds trust.
Trust builds retention.
Retention builds long-term income.

What We Are
We are a healthcare bundle.
We combine:
third-party administered benefits
a nonprofit health sharing membership
Together, those components create a practical healthcare solution.
This is an alternative to traditional insurance.
And we are proud of that.

What We Are Not
We are not:
insurance
a traditional health plan
a replacement for insurance
an insurance company
We are different.
And that is the strength of the model.

Built Different — Built Better
You do not need to apologize for what this is.
You do not need to soften it.
You do not need to hide it.
You explain it clearly.
You explain it confidently.
You explain it honestly.
Because this system works.
And customers respect confidence.

Use the Correct Words
Language matters.
We intentionally use different terms because this is not insurance.
For example:
We say:
Unshared Amount
Not:
Deductible
We say:
Major Health Event
Not:
Major medical claim
We say:
Healthcare Bundle
Not:
Insurance plan
These words are not random.
They are intentional.
Using the correct language protects you and protects the company.

The Sales Truth
Being honest is not a weakness.
It is a sales advantage.
When customers clearly understand what they are buying:
they trust you
they stay longer
they refer others
they become loyal clients
And that is how real businesses grow.

Simple Compliance Rule
If you ever feel unsure what to say:
Tell the truth.
Use the official language.
And explain the model clearly.
That will always keep you safe.

Approved Sales Statements
Here are examples of strong, compliant language you can use.
"This is not traditional insurance — it's a healthcare bundle designed to work differently."
"We're an alternative to traditional insurance, and many people prefer this model because of its flexibility and simplicity."
"The bundle combines everyday healthcare benefits with a health sharing membership for major health events."
"We use the term unshared amount instead of deductible because this is not an insurance plan."
These statements are clear.
These statements are compliant.
These statements build trust.

Statements to Avoid
Never say:
"This is insurance"
"This works just like insurance"
"This replaces insurance"
"This is major medical"
"Your deductible is…"
Even if the customer uses those words, you should gently correct them.

Correcting a Customer Politely
Here is a simple way to handle it.
Customer says:
"So this is insurance, right?"
You respond:
"It's actually a healthcare bundle that works differently than traditional insurance, and I'll walk you through exactly how it works."
That keeps the conversation accurate and professional.

The Real Risk
Compliance mistakes usually do not happen because of bad intentions.
They happen because of sloppy language.
So stay disciplined.
Stay clear.
Stay confident.

The Big Message
You do not need to pretend.
You do not need to imitate insurance.
You do not need to hide the difference.
You simply explain the model.
Clearly.
Confidently.
Honestly.
That is how you sell this the right way.

moduleSteps.title: Step 2 – Instructions
moduleSteps.subtitle: Instructions
moduleSteps.copy:
Review the key compliance principles above.
Pay special attention to:
what we are
what we are not
the correct terminology
the statements to avoid
Keep these rules simple.
Be clear.
Be honest.
Be confident.

moduleSteps.title: Step 3 – Quiz Questions
moduleSteps.subtitle: Take Quiz
moduleSteps.copy:
Module 6 – Compliance & Approved Language
1. True or False
MightyWELL products should be presented as traditional insurance.
Correct Answer:
 False

2. What is the correct way to describe MightyWELL to a customer?
A. A traditional insurance company
 B. A healthcare bundle and alternative to traditional insurance
 C. A government program
Correct Answer:
 B

3. True or False
You should clearly tell customers that this is not insurance.
Correct Answer:
 True

4. Which term should be used instead of deductible?
A. Premium
 B. Unshared Amount
 C. Claim
Correct Answer:
 B

5. True or False
Using the correct terminology helps protect both the agent and the company.
Correct Answer:
 True

6. What should you do if a customer asks if this is insurance?
A. Say yes
 B. Ignore the question
 C. Explain that it is a healthcare bundle that works differently than insurance
Correct Answer:
 C

7. True or False
Being transparent about what the product is helps build customer trust.
Correct Answer:
 True

8. Which of the following statements should be avoided?
A. "This is a healthcare bundle."
 B. "This works exactly like insurance."
 C. "This is an alternative to traditional insurance."
Correct Answer:
 B

9. True or False
Compliance is primarily about being honest and clear with customers.
Correct Answer:
 True

10. What is the safest approach when explaining the product?
A. Use unofficial language
 B. Be vague
 C. Use clear, accurate, approved language
Correct Answer:
 C
